front: Play: George and Martha play games like 'Get the Guests,' 'Hump the Host,' and 'Bringing Up Baby' with Nick and Honey; their son is revealed to be imaginary
back: Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf? (Edward Albee)

front: Play: in the 'Walpurgisnacht' section, a man describes a novel about a blonde professor and his 'Mousie' wife with a 'hysterical pregnancy' to humiliate guests
back: Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf? (Edward Albee)

front: Play: Jerry describes poisoning his landlady's dog with hamburger meat and asks 'Was trying to feed the dog an act of love?' before impaling himself on a knife
Play: a 'permanent transient' describes God as 'a colored queen who wears a kimono and plucks his eyebrows' while on a Central Park bench
Play: human couple Charlie and Nancy meet lizard couple Sarah and Leslie on the beach; punctuated by offstage jet sounds
Play: Harry and Edna flee to Agnes and Tobias's home after becoming suddenly frightened of something they never explain
Play: title characters A, B, and C are revealed to be aspects of the same woman at different ages
Play: wife Stevie drags dead title animal's carcass onstage after learning of her Pritzker Prize-winning architect husband's secret
back: The Goat, or Who is Sylvia? (Edward Albee)
work: The Goat, or Who is Sylvia?

front: Play: Mommy and Daddy adopt a 'bumble of joy' from the Bye-Bye Adoption Service and mutilate it; Grandma is packed off
